title: Eurostat API Vocabulary
description: Domain vocabulary and terminology for the Eurostat Statistics APIs, covering SDMX concepts, data dimensions, and Eurostat-specific terms.

terms.label: Statistical Data and Metadata eXchange
terms.definition: An international initiative designed to standardise the exchange of statistical data and metadata. Eurostat supports both SDMX 2.1 and 3.0.

terms.label: Dataflow
terms.definition: A logical grouping of related data in SDMX, referencing a specific Data Structure Definition. Each Eurostat dataset is accessible via a dataflow identifier (e.g., nama_10_gdp, demo_gind).

terms.label: Dataset Code
terms.definition: The unique identifier for a specific Eurostat statistical dataset, used in the Statistics API. Equivalent to the SDMX dataflow ID.

terms.label: Dimension Key
terms.definition: A dot-separated string specifying dimension values for SDMX data queries. Use '+' to select multiple values in one dimension and leave a dimension empty to wildcard it. The keyword 'all' selects all data.

terms.label: SDMX Flow
terms.definition: Path parameter in SDMX 2.1 data queries that identifies the statistical domain. Can include optional agency prefix and version suffix.

terms.label: Agency Identifier
terms.definition: The identifier of the organisation maintaining the SDMX artefact. Eurostat uses 'ESTAT' as its agency identifier.

terms.label: Resource Identifier
terms.definition: The unique identifier of an SDMX structural artefact such as a dataflow, codelist, concept scheme, or data structure definition.

terms.label: JSON-stat 2.0
terms.definition: A lightweight JSON format for statistical data exchange returned by the Eurostat Statistics API. Encodes multidimensional data with dimension metadata and sparse value arrays.

terms.label: SDMX-ML
terms.definition: XML-based format for SDMX data and structure exchange. Eurostat supports both SDMX-ML 2.1 and 3.0 versions.

terms.label: SDMX-CSV
terms.definition: CSV-based format for SDMX data exchange. Eurostat supports SDMX-CSV 1.0 and 2.0.

terms.label: Start Period
terms.definition: Filter parameter specifying the earliest time period to include in query results. Supports annual (YYYY), quarterly (YYYY-Qn), monthly (YYYY-MM) and weekly (YYYY-Www) formats.

terms.label: End Period
terms.definition: Filter parameter specifying the latest time period to include in query results.

terms.label: First N Observations
terms.definition: SDMX 2.1 parameter to limit results to the N earliest observations per series.

terms.label: Last N Observations
terms.definition: SDMX 2.1 parameter to limit results to the N most recent observations per series.

terms.label: Detail Level
terms.definition: Controls the level of detail in SDMX 2.1 data responses.

terms.label: References
terms.definition: Controls which referenced artefacts are included in SDMX structure responses.

terms.label: Async Request UUID
terms.definition: Universally unique identifier assigned to an asynchronous data extraction request. Used to poll status and retrieve results.

terms.label: Asynchronous Request Status
terms.definition: The processing state of an asynchronous data extraction request.

values.description: Data is ready and available for download
Data was processed but cache has expired

terms.label: Codelist
terms.definition: An SDMX artefact that defines a set of coded values for a dimension or attribute. For example, CL_GEO contains country and region codes.

terms.label: Data Structure Definition (DSD)
terms.definition: An SDMX artefact that defines the structure of a dataset, specifying the dimensions, attributes, and measures.

terms.label: Concept Scheme
terms.definition: An SDMX artefact that defines a set of concepts used as dimensions, attributes, or measures in data structures.

terms.label: DCAT
terms.definition: W3C Data Catalog Vocabulary. Eurostat exposes its dataset catalogue in DCAT-AP (Application Profile for data portals in Europe) RDF format.

terms.label: Metabase
terms.definition: A gzipped text file catalogue containing definitions of all disseminated Eurostat data products, available at /catalogue/metabase.txt.gz.

terms.label: Geographic Dimension
terms.definition: Standard Eurostat dimension representing the reporting geographic entity. Common values include EU country codes (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2), EU aggregates (EU27_2020, EA20), and NUTS regions.

terms.label: Nomenclature of Territorial Units for Statistics
terms.definition: The hierarchical classification system for EU regions used in Eurostat data. NUTS 0 = countries, NUTS 1 = major regions, NUTS 2 = basic regions, NUTS 3 = small regions.

terms.label: European System of National Accounts 2010
terms.definition: The EU standard for national accounts, defining concepts like GDP (B1GQ), gross value added, and household consumption used in Eurostat macroeconomic datasets.

terms.label: Since Time Period
terms.definition: Filter parameter in the Statistics API specifying the start of the time range. Returns all observations from this period onwards.

terms.label: Last N Time Periods
terms.definition: Filter parameter in the Statistics API requesting only the most recent N time periods.

terms.label: Extraction Type
terms.definition: Parameter for the Catalogue DCAT endpoint controlling which datasets are included.
